blocks.body: Adjectives and Adverbs is a compact section about comparison and the difference between adjectives and adverbs.

blocks.heading: Adjectives
blocks.body:
• Adjectives in this topic help you compare things and choose between an adjective and an adverb.
  • You will work with forms such as fast, faster, the fastest and contrasts like good / well.
  • Examples: a fast car, a more careful answer, as tall as his brother.

blocks.heading: Adverbs
blocks.body:
• Adverbs appear here in the lesson Adjectives vs Adverbs and in comparison patterns.
  • The focus is on what the form describes, how it is built, and where it stands in a sentence.
  • Examples: She works carefully. He runs faster.

blocks.heading: Degrees of Comparison
blocks.body:
• Degrees of Comparison are the core of this mini-section.
  • Separate lessons explain comparative vs superlative and as... as / not as... as.
  • Together they form a compact learning path around comparison.

blocks.heading: Topics in This Section
blocks.body:
TopicLevelDescription
Degrees of ComparisonA2–B1Building comparative and superlative forms
Comparative vs SuperlativeA2–B1Choosing the right form for the context
Equality ComparisonA2–B1as... as / not as... as
Adjectives vs AdverbsA2–B1What an adjective describes and what an adverb describes

blocks.heading: Useful Pointers
blocks.body:
• Degrees of Comparison gives you the basic system of comparatives and superlatives.
  • Comparative vs Superlative helps you choose the correct form in context.
  • Equality Comparison covers patterns such as as... as / not as... as.
  • Adjectives vs Adverbs helps you avoid mistakes like good / well.

blocks.heading: Study Tips
blocks.body:
🧠

Start with Degrees of Comparison, then move to Comparative vs Superlative and Equality Comparison.

Adjectives vs Adverbs clears up a common problem with good / well, -ly forms, and word position.
